The selected item is marked by a SHORT CENTERED accent pill flush with the item bottom (not a full-width underline, not bold); text stays TextPrimary at rest for every item and DIMS on hover/press — backgrounds are transparent in every state (SelectorBar_themeresources.xaml:21-25). Stateless — the caller owns selected and reacts to onSelect. Keyboard: ONE tab stop (the selected item — TabNavigation=Once, SelectorBar.xaml:13); Left/Right/ Home/End move focus item-to-item and SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS (SelectorBarTests.cs:63-66); focus entering a bar without a selection auto-selects (SelectorBar.cpp:98-126). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see TemplateParts for the contract).

SelectorVisuals

The SelectorVisual presets as ItemContainerFactory-shaped builders: each returns the WinUI item chrome for one preset around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate, signature-identical to the ItemContainerFactory delegate (ItemsView.cs:52-54) so a preset is just ContainerFactory = SelectorVisuals.<Name>.

Each builder is PLAIN/recyclable — a keyed BoxEl tree with NO Component / no reactive bind (TransformBind/OpacityBind/FillBind/WidthBind/HeightBind/OnRealized would break TreeReconciler.IsRecyclable, Reconciler.cs:748-749); Animate (a LayoutTransition) is allowed because it is declarative. Per-item chrome SKIN goes through the ContainerFactory/SelectorVisual seam; per-item VARIATION goes through the PartDelta value seam (fill/fg/opacity/corner/padding/glyph as values, applied during construction — shape-stable, 0-alloc, CI-enforced; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). WinUI chrome values are cited inline; they are re-verified against the live ListViewBaseItemChrome.cpp metrics (s_selectionIndicatorSize {3,16}, s_selectionIndicatorHeightShrinkage 6, s_selectionIndicatorMargin.left 4, s_backplateMargin {4,2}, s_multiSelectRoundedSquareInlineMargin 14, s_multiSelectRoundedContentOffset 28, s_multiSelectSquareOverlayMargin {0,2,2,0}) and the per-state brush ramps in {ListView,GridView,ItemContainer}_themeresources.xaml.

Slider

A WinUI 3 Slider. Geometry (Slider_themeresources.xaml): a 4px rail (SliderTrackFill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault, radius 2) with an accent value-fill, and the Fluent thumb — a STATIC 22px ring (the 18px Thumb part + its Border's −2 margin, lines 169-172 + 198) of ControlSolidFillColorDefault with a 1px ControlElevationBorder, holding the only animated part: the 12px accent inner dot whose storyboards scale it 0.86 at rest / 1.167 on hover / 0.71 on press / 1.167 disabled (lines 204-254). Value-fill and inner dot recolor with the CONTROL-wide PointerOver/Pressed states (AccentFillColorDefault → Secondary → Tertiary; lines 14-17, 24-27).

  • Create(float, Action<float>, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?)/Bind(FloatSignal, Action<float>?, float, float, Style?, bool, string?, TemplateParts?) — the lightweight 0..1 scrub primitives (media seek/volume). Keyboard, focus visuals and the optional header are included; the value tooltip is not (see Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?)).
  • Ranged(float, Action<float>, Options, float, float, Style?, bool, TemplateParts?) — the full WinUI-parity control over Slider.Options: arbitrary range, step snapping, ticks (incl. inline), Header, SmallChange/LargeChange keyboard, and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled "disambiguation UI" value tooltip with a ThumbToolTipValueConverter-equivalent formatter.

Keyboard (KeyPress::Slider::KeyDown, SliderKeyProcess.h:28-71): Left/Down decrease and Right/Up increase by SmallChange, Home/End jump to Minimum/Maximum; PageUp/PageDown move by LargeChange (not in XAML's KeyPress::Slider — kept per the parity contract, riding the same Slider::Step math, Slider_Partial.cpp:1713-1783). RTL note: WinUI inverts Left/Right by (FlowDirection==RightToLeft) ^ IsDirectionReversed (SliderKeyProcess.h:26); the engine has no FlowDirection yet — the LTR mapping ships now, the inversion lands with the Wave-6 RTL sweep.

Slider.Options

Extended slider configuration: range, step snapping, ticks, orientation, header, keyboard step sizes and the thumb value tooltip ("disambiguation UI").

Slider.Style
SplitButton

A WinUI 3 SplitButton: a primary action button joined to a secondary dropdown half, sharing one rounded chrome with a 1px divider between them. The two halves are independently interactive — the primary half runs OnInvoke (the Click event / OnClickPrimary), the secondary half opens a MenuFlyout anchored below the whole control (OnClickSecondary → OpenFlyout). Each half carries its own hover/press fill + foreground ramp; the inner corner where each half meets the divider is squared so the pair reads as one control (WinUI PrimaryButtonBorder 4,0,0,4 / SecondaryButtonBorder 0,4,4,0).

Source of truth: microsoft-ui-xaml/controls/dev/SplitButton/SplitButton.xaml (+ _themeresources.xaml, SplitButton.cpp). Tokens, sizes, padding, corners and the state matrix are 1:1 with that template.

SplitView

A WinUI SplitView: a side pane and a flexible content area, composed per SplitViewDisplayMode × PanePlacement × IsPaneOpen (the WinUI visual-state table, SplitView_Partial.cpp:34-59):

<ul><li><b>Overlay</b> (the WinUI default) — the open pane floats over the content without reflow (PaneRoot
Canvas.ZIndex=1 over a ColumnSpan=2 ContentRoot, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700, :718) and light-dismisses on
content click / Escape / a host resize, with the WinUI 0.35s/0.12s slide.</li><li><b>Inline</b> — the open pane reserves <code>paneWidth</code> of layout; the content pre-slides in sync
(ContentTransform, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:345-348) over the 0.2s/0.1s inline curves.</li><li><b>CompactOverlay / CompactInline</b> — closed leaves a <code>compactPaneLength</code> (48) rail: the SAME pane,
clipped to the rail (a clip-reveal, never a resize — core SplitView.cpp:343-377).</li></ul>

State is caller-owned (<code class="paramref">isPaneOpen</code> is a prop, the WinUI <code>IsPaneOpen</code> DP): wire
<code class="paramref">setIsPaneOpen</code> so light dismiss can write the close back. PaneOpening/PaneOpened/
PaneClosing(cancelable on light dismiss)/PaneClosed mirror the WinUI events (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:82-96).
SplitViewPaneClosingArgs

WinUI SplitViewPaneClosingEventArgs (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:31-39): set Cancel to keep the pane open. Honored on LIGHT-DISMISS closes only (core SplitView.cpp:212-244 TryCloseLightDismissiblePane → CSplitViewPaneClosingExecutor:27-38); an app-initiated close raises the event but ignores Cancel (core SplitView.cpp:432-456).

SwipeAction

One swipe action (WinUI SwipeItem — SwipeControl.idl:69-91): an icon glyph, a label, an optional custom background Color (SwipeItem.Background — flips the content to on-accent text), the OnInvoked callback (the Invoked event / Command) and BehaviorOnInvoked.

SwipeControl

A WinUI SwipeControl (controls\dev\SwipeControl): drag the content sideways to reveal action items underneath. The content pans with the pointer; releasing past min(items width, 100px) rests it open (c_ThresholdValue — SwipeControl.cpp:21, ConfigurePositionInertiaRestingValues :937-1005), otherwise it springs closed; an already-open side closes on ANY inward movement (UpdateThresholdReached, :1626-1645). Items are invokable (Invoked + BehaviorOnInvoked); Execute mode stretches one item across the control and auto-invokes it on open. Esc and a tap on the content dismiss an open swipe (AttachDismissingHandlers, SwipeControl.h:74-76). Horizontal (Left/Right) items only — WinUI's vertical Top/Bottom axis is not offered on this surface, so its "both axes populated" rejection (ThrowIfHasVerticalAndHorizontalContent, SwipeControl.cpp:1648-1676) cannot arise. Deliberate superset: WinUI only swipes on touch/pen (VisualInteractionSource); ours pans with the mouse too.

TabView

A WinUI TabView (controls\dev\TabView): a horizontal strip of tab headers atop the selected tab's content. The control OWNS its live tab collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135), seeded once from Items/Tabs at mount: the close button / middle-click / Ctrl+F4 remove the tab (after raising OnTabCloseRequested) with WinUI's reselect-at-the-removed-index rule (TabView.cpp:786-812); the add button appends what OnAddTabButtonClick returns; pointer drag reorders tabs (CanReorderTabs, default true). Equal width mode clamps tabs to 100–240px of the measured strip; overflowing tabs scroll behind 32×24 repeat scroll buttons (±50px, disabled at the extremes) and the selected tab is auto-scrolled into view. The selected header uses the SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary surface and fuses flush with the content area (the strip's 1px CardStroke baseline is per-tab and omitted under the selected header). Per-part restyling goes through Parts (see TemplateParts for the contract).

TabViewItem

One tab (WinUI TabViewItem, TabView.idl:205-230): a Header string, an optional leading icon glyph (the IconSource slot, idl:214-215), per-tab IsClosable (default true, idl:217-219), and a Content factory rendered in the content area while this tab is selected.

TeachingTip

WinUI TeachingTip (controls/dev/TeachingTip/TeachingTip.xaml + _themeresources + .cpp/.idl): an anchored (targeted) or window-relative (untargeted) non-modal callout that teaches/highlights a feature. The body is a SOLID surface — SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary background, SurfaceStrokeColorDefault 1px border, OverlayCornerRadius (8), an elevation shadow, and the 1px TeachingTipTopHighlightBrush top highlight (#0DFFFFFF dark / #99FFFFFF light, TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml:6/:28) — with (top→bottom) an optional hero image, an icon + title + subtitle row, the main content, and a footer button panel (Action + Close). A targeted tip grows a 16×8 tail/beak that points at the target; an untargeted tip has no tail. NOT light-dismiss by default (IsLightDismissEnabled=false): only the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close dismisses it.

The FULL 13-placement matrix (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates :115-264) is honored — Top/Bottom/Left/Right, the 8 corner variants, and Center — each mapping to the matching FlyoutPositioner placement with the tail pinned to the joined edge per the state's alignment setters (e.g. TopRight → tail at the LEFT of the bottom edge, :160-170). PlacementMargin nudges the tip away from the target (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 offset); ShouldConstrainToRootBounds (default true, the WinUI idl default) opts the popup out of the window bounds via the E4 windowed-popup seam when false.

1:1 tokens/sizes (TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml, unitless = epx): • MinWidth 320, MaxWidth 336, MinHeight 40, MaxHeight 520 (TeachingTipMin/MaxWidth/Height). • Content margin 12 on all sides (TeachingTipContentMargin); button panel top margin 0,12,0,0 (TeachingTipButtonPanelMargin); both-buttons split — Action 0,12,4,0 (Left), Close 4,12,0,0 (Right). • MainContent present-margin 0,12,0,0 (gap below the title block when content exists); icon→title gap 12 (TeachingTipIconPresenterMarginWithIcon); title-stack right margin 28 when the header (alternate) close button shows. • Title FontSize 16 SemiBold; Subtitle FontSize 14; body content FontSize 14 (Foreground = TextPrimary). • Alternate (header) close button: 40×40 (TeachingTipAlternateCloseButtonSize), E711 glyph at 16px, SubtleFill ramp, ControlCornerRadius. The footer ActionButton's DEFAULT style is the STANDARD button (TeachingTip.xaml:10 ActionButtonStyle = DefaultButtonStyle) — set ActionButtonIsAccent for the AccentButtonStyle the gallery samples use.

Open/close motion (host-driven, PopupChrome.TeachingTip): expand scale → 1 over 300ms cubic-bezier(0.1,0.9,0.2,1); contract scale → 20/Width over 200ms cubic-bezier(0.7,0,1,0.5) (TeachingTip.cpp:1660-1712; .h:234-235 + :304-307 control points) — both at full opacity (the storyboards carry no opacity keyframes).

TeachingTip.ClosingEventArgs

WinUI TeachingTipClosingEventArgs (synchronous Cancel slice; async Deferral is deep-deferred).

TextBlocks

WinUI TextBlock equivalents: read-only styled text helpers mirroring the WinUI type ramp (Title / Subtitle / Body / BodyStrong / Caption). Display only — no input.

TextBox

WinUI TextBox: a text field built on EditableText, optionally with a header label stacked above and a description row below (the WinUI template's HeaderContentPresenter / DescriptionPresenter, TextBox_themeresources.xaml:335/340). Header-less it is just the bare field. EditableText already supplies the bordered surface (fill, gradient ControlElevationBorder, focus affordance, 4px corners) — TextBox wraps no second border. Single-line shows the DeleteButton (✕ E894 while focused ∧ non-empty); multi-line (acceptsReturn) hides it (WinUI: the DeleteButton is suppressed when TextWrapping/AcceptsReturn make the box multi-line).

TimePicker

A WinUI TimePicker: a single Button FACE split into Hour | Minute | AM/PM text columns (equal star widths, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:234-239) separated by 1px vertical spacers, and on tap opens ONE TimePickerFlyout presenter via the Overlay service (the whole face is a single FlyoutButton, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:232). The popup is placed so the centered 40px accent highlight band overlays the face (DateTimePickerFlyoutHelper.cpp:39-71) and is width-matched to the face's realized width (TimePickerFlyout_Partial.cpp:196-200). It hosts side-by-side LOOPING selector columns (item height 40) — the shared FluentGpu.Controls.DateTimeLoopColumn — under the highlight band, with a 41px Accept(E8FB) / Dismiss(E711) bar. SelectedTime is nullable (null shows the hour/minute/AM placeholders, the WinUI HasNoTime state); ClockIdentifier selects the 12/24-hour clock and MinuteIncrement coarsens the minute column.

TitleBar

The WinUI 3 TitleBar control (WinAppSDK 1.7, microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\TitleBar) over a custom frame (CustomFrame): back + pane-toggle buttons (40w, 16px glyphs), a 16×16 app-identity icon, 12px Caption title/subtitle, a centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox), and — unlike WinUI, which reserves space for SHELL-drawn caption buttons — three ENGINE-drawn min/max/close CaptionButtons (46w, full bar height, close = shell red) so the whole bar is one GPU-rendered surface.

Non-client plumbing: the control captures its part handles (OnRealized) and, in a layout effect (after layout, before paint — push-on-relayout only, never per frame), reports TitleBarRegions through SetTitleBarRegions: interactive islands (back/pane/content) FIRST as Client, then the three button rects (→ HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE — the Win11 snap-layouts flyout requires HTMAXBUTTON), then the whole bar as the catch-all Caption drag band (first match wins in WM_NCHITTEST). Window activation/placement changes arrive via the host-bumped WindowChromeEpoch signal: deactivation dims title→tertiary, icon/content→50% opacity, caption glyphs→disabled (the WinUI Deactivated visual state); maximize re-glyphs max↔restore.

ToggleButton

A two/three-state toggle (shuffle/repeat/follow). Accent-filled when on — WinUI ToggleButton's checked state (AccentFillColorDefault bg + TextOnAccent fg + AccentControlElevationBorder); the unchecked AND indeterminate states are a standard control fill. Controlled: pass the state and a toggle/cycle callback. Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-61, Light :120-177; DefaultToggleButtonStyle :180-363). The full WinUI matrix is wired: {Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate} × {Normal, PointerOver, Pressed, Disabled} for fill, foreground and border, verified in both themes. State flips (checked↔unchecked) cross-fade over the WinUI BrushTransition 83ms (ContentPresenter.BackgroundTransition, ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:199-201) — the E3 primitive. No scale animation: WinUI state storyboards are color-only (lines 202-357).

ToggleButton.Style
ToggleSplitButton

A WinUI ToggleSplitButton (SplitButton + checked state). Like SplitButton it is one joined chrome with two independently-clickable halves — a primary half on the left and a dropdown half on the right separated by a 1px divider — but the primary half toggles on/off instead of running a one-shot action: clicking it (or Enter/Space activation) flips IsChecked and raises OnToggle; the dropdown half opens a MenuFlyout. WinUI derives ToggleSplitButton from SplitButton and they share one ControlTemplate; the checked visual-states are driven by InternalIsChecked() (SplitButton.cpp UpdateVisualStates) — so this control is the SplitButton chrome with the accent Checked* token ramps folded in.

When checked, both halves fill accent (SplitButtonBackgroundChecked = AccentFillColorDefault), the foreground is TextOnAccent, the outer border is AccentControlElevationBorder, and the inner divider switches to the on-accent divider stroke. Open/close goes through the overlay popup service (Service): re-click toggles, Escape / click-outside light-dismiss, focus captured on open + restored on close (host-wired), FocusTrap roves the menu, and ExpandCollapse Collapsed↔Expanded is raised by the overlay lifecycle. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle the primary (the engine's focused-clickable activation), Down opens the flyout. IsEnabled gates all interaction via the engine and folds the chrome to the disabled tokens. The checked state is a caller Signal<T> so a page can read/display it; pass OnToggle to observe flips.

ToggleSwitch

A WinUI ToggleSwitch — geometry/colors/motion from the shipped lifted style (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleSwitch_themeresources.xaml, "the template"): a 40×20 pill track (OuterBorder/SwitchKnobBounds, template:507-508) + a knob inside a 20×20 positioning host (SwitchKnob grid, template:509) that translates 0→20 on toggle (KnobTranslateTransform To=20, template:445). The knob is 12×12 at rest (template:510/515 + Normal storyboard :231-242), grows to 14×14 on hover (:268-279), and STRETCHES to 17×14 pinned 3px from the near edge while pressed (size :311-322; the Pressed VisualState.Setters pin SwitchKnobOn right / SwitchKnobOff left with a 3px margin, :284-287). All knob size/anchor changes tween 83ms ControlFasterAnimationDuration with the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) (Common_themeresources_any.xaml:602/606 — FluentPopOpen); the disabled return is 250ms ControlNormalAnimationDuration (:357-368); knob travel is WinUI's RepositionThemeAnimation (:418-439), mapped per the repo motion canon to ControlFast = 167ms (Dsl/Motion.cs — the PVL reposition timing is system data, not in the XAML).

Behavior follows dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp: tap toggles (TapHandler :874-905); DRAG-TO-TOGGLE — the knob follows the pointer's horizontal delta clamped to the travel (DragDeltaHandler/MoveDelta :823-841/:579-589, SetTranslations clamp :455-457) and on release toggles iff the knob crossed half the travel range (MoveCompleted :591-619); pointer loss mid-drag cleans up without toggling (OnPointerCaptureLost :728-746). Space toggles via the engine's Space-on-keyup activation → OnClick (HandlesKey = Space/GamepadA only, :1002-1007); arrow/Home/End keys toggle directionally (ToggleSwitchKeyProcess.h:52-71 — WinUI acts on KeyUp; the engine routes OnKeyDown, a known timing-only deviation). Controlled — the caller owns isOn.

ToggleSwitch.Style
ToolTip

A WinUI ToolTip (framework ToolTip + ToolTipService): wraps a target element and surfaces a small text bubble anchored above it (or at the pointer in Mouse). 1:1 with the framework's automatic-tooltip behavior:

  • Hover trigger — pointer-over the target opens the bubble after the initial show delay (SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME 400ms × 2 = 800ms; a re-show within BetweenShowDelayMs = 200ms uses the reshow delay — see MouseReshowDelayMs for why that is 400ms, not the spec'd 600ms). Pointer-leave cancels a PENDING open immediately (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1435-1442); an OPEN bubble is kept and closed by the safe-zone monitor instead — see the safe-zone item below.
  • Safe zone — an open bubble does NOT close the instant the pointer leaves the target: WinUI keeps it while the pointer is inside owner ∪ tooltip (∪ their convex hull) and a 1s check timer closes it once outside (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1433-1453 owner-exit only records the owner; cpp:349-381 OnSafeZoneCheck; cpp:1060-1098 IsToolTipInSafeZone; .h:22 s_safeZoneCheckTimerDuration = 1s). The engine surfaces no global pointer position, so the hull is approximated by the same 1s cadence: leaving owner ∪ bubble arms a SafeZoneCheckMs grace; re-entering either cancels it; the grace elapsing closes the bubble.
  • Keyboard trigger — KEYBOARD focus landing inside the target opens the bubble after 800ms (×2, normal AND reshow: ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1777-1779), exactly WinUI's OnOwnerGotFocus gate (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1648-1664: only FocusState::Keyboard shows the tooltip — pointer focus does not). Focus leaving closes it (cpp:1696-1706 OnOwnerLostFocus → OnOwnerLeaveInternal).
  • Press dismiss — a pointer press over the target closes an open (or pending) bubble, and it stays closed until the pointer LEAVES and re-enters (classic tooltip behavior; WinUI 3's ToolTipService itself registers no PointerPressed handler — it closes via safe-zone exit monitoring, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1437-1453 — but cannot re-open a dismissed owner until a real leave + re-enter either, cpp:725-737 CancelAutomaticToolTip).
  • Auto-dismiss — an open bubble closes itself after ShowDurationMs (SPI_GETMESSAGEDURATION default 5s), and the dismissal LATCHES like press dismiss: WinUI's only show trigger is PointerEntered (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1395-1418), so in-place hover moves can never re-open a timed-out tooltip — it takes a real leave + re-enter.
  • No click trigger — the wrapper adds NO OnClick (ToolTipService registers none, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:176-220), so a tooltipped element is never a tab stop by itself.

Chrome matches ToolTip_themeresources.xaml: AcrylicInAppFillColorDefault fill, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorFlyout stroke, ControlCornerRadius (4px) corners, the light tooltip elevation (Tooltip), 9,6,9,8 padding, 12px TextFillColorPrimary text, MaxWidth 320, TextWrapping=Wrap. Open/close are the WinUI FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation fades (167ms — Raw in the overlay host). The bubble does NOT trap focus and does NOT light-dismiss on outside click (None) — dismissal is owned by hover-leave/focus-leave/press + the auto-dismiss timer, exactly like ToolTipService.

TreeNode

One node in a TreeView: a label plus zero or more child nodes. IsEnabled gates interaction (disabled rows dim to 0.3); custom row content comes from the tree's ItemTemplate.

TreeView
Tween

The typed computed-"TemplateSettings" convention — the antidote to WinUI's stringly TemplateSettings bound into storyboards. A control declares a local readonly record struct <Control>TemplateSettings(...) of typed values (rotations, heights, clip rects, open/close animation positions), computes it ONCE per state change (a pure factory called in render, or a UseMemo/OnRealized effect that reads the control's own signals), and feeds the fields into the engine's existing channels: static props, TransformBind/OpacityBind/WidthBind/ HeightBind, AnimEngine keyframe seeds, or the clip-rect channel (AnimChannel.ClipL/T/R/B). Never rebuild the record inside a hot bind/effect body — compute it in the memo/factory. ExpanderTemplateSettings is the reference; the same shape serves CommandBarFlyout, NavigationView, Progress*, TeachingTip, TabView, TreeView, … These small helpers are the shared interpolation math those records use.

VariableSizedWrapGrid

A WinUI VariableSizedWrapGrid: a grid that wraps tiles of varying sizes. This is a self-contained, non-virtualized demo — fixed-base cell tiles where some span multiple cells. Tiles are greedily packed left-to-right into rows that hold columns cells (by ColSpan sum); a tile that would overflow the current row starts a new one. Cells are contiguous (no inter-cell gap) — an N-span tile is exactly N*cell — matching the WinUI base panel, which is an unstyled layout host (no per-tile chrome). A faint fill is kept only for visibility.

Viewbox

A WinUI Viewbox: scales its single child by a composited transform (no relayout). WinUI computes the scale from a layout measure pass; this engine has no measure-override seam on BoxEl, so the scale is supplied either as an explicit factor (the scale overload) or computed from a known content size and target box via ViewboxStretch/ViewboxStretchDirection logic (the sized overload). The child is wrapped in a centered BoxEl carrying a uniform ScaleX/ScaleY.

Virtual

Fluent factories for virtualized collections. All recycle over the slab free-list and keep a 0-alloc in-window scroll. Custom(int, IVirtualLayout, Func<int, Element>, Func<int, string>?, int, bool) takes any IVirtualLayout you implement. The returned VirtualListEl record lives in FluentGpu.Reconciler (the reconciler diffs it directly).

WrapTile

One tile in a VariableSizedWrapGrid: a label plus how many base cells it spans horizontally (ColSpan) and vertically (RowSpan).

Structs

ExpanderTemplateSettings

Computed template settings for the Expander (the typed-record convention — see Tween): the chevron rotation and whether the content panel participates, derived once from the open state. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's generated ExpanderTemplateSettings binds into its chevron storyboard.

InfoBadgeTemplateSettings

Computed template settings for the InfoBadge — the typed-record convention modelled on ExpanderTemplateSettings. Mirrors WinUI's generated InfoBadgeTemplateSettings: the display kind (Dot vs Value vs Icon, derived from Value/Icon exactly like OnDisplayKindPropertiesChanged), the resolved badge height/width and the pill corner radius (= ActualHeight/2, per OnSizeChanged), and the per-kind content margin.

InfoBar.InfoBarTemplateSettings

The TemplateSettings convention (per the audit's P3) - the per-severity computed geometry WinUI binds into its severity VisualState: the standard status glyph, the icon-background fill (SystemFillColorBrush), the inverse glyph foreground, and the tinted content-root background (SystemFillColorBackgroundBrush). Computed once per severity by the pure factory For(InfoBarSeverity) - no per-frame allocation.

ItemChromeState

Per-item visual state handed to a custom ItemContainerFactory (the L4 skin seam).

ItemCollectionTransition

WinUI ItemCollectionTransition mapped onto the engine's FLIP/LayoutTransition pipeline (E11-L2): Moves = position FLIP (a reorder/removal slides the survivors), Adds = enter fade-in, Removes = exit fade-out, all over WinUI's ControlFastAnimationDuration 167ms / decelerate spline (0,0,0,1) — the timing the ItemContainer/Repeater storyboards use (ItemContainer.xaml:54-56 KeySpline="0,0,0,1" at ControlFastAnimationDuration). Virtualized caveat (documented, deliberate): enter transitions also play when an item SCROLLS into the realized window as a fresh mount — WinUI's LinedFlow behaves the same way for newly realized elements.

MenuFlyoutItem

A single row in a MenuFlyout: a label, an optional leading glyph (icon column), an enabled flag, the action to run when chosen, and an optional trailing keyboard-accelerator hint (e.g. "Ctrl+S"). Use Separator for a divider, Toggle(string, bool, Action?, string?, bool) for a checkable item (E73E check column), RadioItem(string, bool, Action?, string?, bool) for a mutually-exclusive choice (E915 bullet), or SubMenu(string, IReadOnlyList<MenuFlyoutItem>, string?, bool) for a cascading sub-menu (E974 chevron column, hover/Right-arrow opens the nested popup). All non-Command extras are optional so the primary (Label, Glyph, Enabled, Invoke) constructor call-sites are unchanged.

OverlayPlacementInfo

Resolved popup placement in popup-local coordinates, updated by OverlayHost after layout.

PartDelta

Per-item VARIATION as VALUES (the newly-legal per-item-customization seam — supersedes the banned per-item TemplateParts modifier in recycled scroll paths). Every field is a nullable override: null ⇒ keep the preset default. Applied DURING chrome construction (a plain with-swap into the already-allocated BoxEl/TextEl record), so per-item variation costs ZERO extra allocation and is provably shape-stable (the realizer shape-hash guard + a steady-scroll HotPhaseAllocBytes==0 check enforce it; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). The producing Func must be pure-value: no new/box/LINQ/Animate per call.

PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings

Computed geometry/content WinUI bakes into PersonPictureTemplateSettings + its size-driven font rules. Pure factory (no signals): the resolved initials/glyph string, which face renders it, and the size-derived font/badge metrics — fed straight into the static element props (this is a cold, reconcile-time composition).

PopupOptions

Optional popup behavior. FocusTrap keeps Tab/Shift-Tab inside the overlay subtree (modal-style).

PopupPlacementResult

Result of placing an anchored popup: the absolute top-left, which side it opened on (so the open animation can reveal from the correct edge), the width/height that actually fit (after a full collision resize), the EFFECTIVE placement after fallback (WinUI PerformPlacementWithFallback reports the placement that won), and the corner-join against the anchor.

ProgressBar.ProgressBarTemplateSettings

The computed translate positions WinUI binds from ProgressBarTemplateSettings into the indeterminate storyboards (ProgressBar.cpp UpdateWidthBasedTemplateSettings). Indicator widths follow SetProgressBarIndicatorWidth.

ProgressRing.ProgressRingTemplateSettings

Computed geometry for one ProgressRing (the audit's P3 TemplateSettings convention — mirrors WinUI's ProgressRingTemplateSettings, recomputed once from the control's size, never inside a per-frame bind). Sweep is the determinate accent-arc sweep in degrees (value·360). For the indeterminate ring it is the fixed visible arc length the rotation loop spins.

RatingControlTemplateSettings

Computed template settings for the RatingControl (the typed-record convention -- see PersonPicture.PersonPictureTemplateSettings / Expander.ExpanderTemplateSettings): the resolved display value (the rating shown right now -- pointer-over preview, else committed value, else placeholder), the foreground/background brushes for the current visual state, and the partial-star clip width baked for the foreground "Set" layer. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's RatingControl::UpdateRatingItemsAppearance derives each pass and binds into its two clipped stack panels. Pure factory: no signals are read here -- the caller passes the already-resolved inputs, so this struct is cheap to build per render and never allocates in a hot bind.

The star foreground recolor is INSTANT, never eased: WinUI drives every star visual state with VisualStateManager::GoToState(useTransitions:false) (see UpdateRatingItemsAppearance), so the brush steps the instant the pointer-over preview resolves. We honor that by computing the resting Foreground directly from the sweep and leaving the glyph's HoverColor at default (A==0 ⇒ the recorder leaves the color untouched and runs no hover ramp) -- a granular re-render swaps the resting color in one step, no cross-fade.

RepeatLayout

A pluggable layout for ItemsRepeater(int, Func<int, Element>, in RepeatLayout, Func<int, string>?, int, Action<int>?, Action<int>?, Action<int, int>?, Action<int, int>?, ItemCollectionTransition?) — the equivalent of WinUI's Layout object. A readonly struct (no per-call allocation). Stack/Grid/Custom virtualize (only the window is realized); Wrap/Inline are non-virtual (build the whole child list — use for SMALL collections like a nav pane or a toolbar). Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/HorizontalGrid ride the Custom slot (every layout object IS an IVirtualLayout; variable-extent ones are IMeasuredVirtualLayout — E11-L0's one seam). NOTE: the stateful layouts (Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/Grouped) own realize tables — when the OWNING component re-renders, hoist the layout instance (e.g. UseMemo) instead of calling the factory inline each render.

RichTextBlock.Options

Per-block options (WinUI DP equivalents). Defaults match the WinUI control: selection enabled (RichTextBlock.cpp:1730), theme selection brush, the established 560px reading measure + 10px paragraph gap.

StateBrush

Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as Animate on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens.

Interfaces

IOverlayService

Enums

AnnotatedScrollBarScrollKind

Why an AnnotatedScrollBar scrolled — mirrors WinUI AnnotatedScrollBarScrollingEventKind (AnnotatedScrollBar.idl:6-12).

AppBarCommandKind

The kind of a AppBarCommand in a CommandBarFlyout/CommandBar: a plain push button, a checkable toggle (PRIMARY toggles paint the accent pill when checked; OVERFLOW toggles show only the E73E check glyph — V2 OverflowChecked, CommandBarFlyout_themeresources.xaml:434-438), or a divider row.

BackgroundSizing

WinUI BackgroundSizing: whether the control fill stops at the inner border edge or extends under the border to its outer edge. WinUI DefaultButtonStyle = InnerBorderEdge, AccentButtonStyle = OuterBorderEdge (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\Button_themeresources.xaml:156,238); ToggleButton flips InnerBorderEdge → OuterBorderEdge while checked (ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:182,6). Carried on the button-family styles as the API/parity contract. HONEST NOTE: the renderer today draws every fill to the OUTER rounded-rect edge with the border stroked on top — i.e. OuterBorderEdge-equivalent for all values; the 1px fill inset of InnerBorderEdge is a named E9 render-primitive gap, not silently dropped. With the WinUI hairline (1px) translucent elevation borders the visual delta is sub-pixel-class.

CalendarViewDisplayMode

WinUI CalendarViewDisplayMode — the zoom level: Month (day grid) / Year (4-column months) / Decade (4-column years).

CalendarViewSelectionMode

WinUI CalendarViewSelectionMode: None / Single / Multiple (controls.idl).

CheckState

The tri-state of a CheckBox / ToggleButton: cleared, set, or mixed (indeterminate).

CommandBarDisplayMode

WinUI CommandBar.ClosedDisplayMode: how the CLOSED bar renders — Compact (48px icon strip), Minimal (24px sliver with only the … More button), Hidden (collapsed).

CommandBarFlyoutShowMode

WinUI FlyoutShowMode subset for CommandBarFlyout (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:162-201): Standard opens with the overflow ALREADY expanded — transitions suppressed while opening (m_commandBarFlyoutIsOpening → UpdateUI(false), CommandBarFlyoutCommandBar.cpp:25/:121/:189-197) — and focuses the first command (cpp:29-71). Transient (the default) opens collapsed and never steals focus. AlwaysExpanded forces Standard (CommandBarFlyout.cpp:173-177).

ContentDialog.DefaultBtn
ContentDialogResult

How a ContentDialog was dismissed (WinUI ContentDialogResult): the primary/secondary command, or None (close button / Escape / programmatic / light-of-modal never applies).

CornerJoin

Which popup corners abut (and so should square against) the anchor edge — WinUI UpdateCornerRadius corner-joining for ComboBox/AutoSuggestBox/DropDownButton, where the dropdown reads as one piece with the field.

DismissBehavior

Opens anchored, light-dismissable flyouts (menus, ComboBox/DropDownButton/SplitButton dropdowns, ColorPicker). A control captures its own node via OnRealized and passes a thunk for it as the anchor; the host positions the popup relative to the anchor's on-screen rect (flip/nudge into the viewport) and animates it open/closed with the WinUI MenuPopupThemeTransition. Resolve via UseContext(Overlay.Service) inside an OverlayHost.

FlyoutPlacement

Where a flyout opens relative to its anchor — the full WinUI FlyoutPlacementMode matrix (FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:84-110 maps each mode to a major side + an edge justification) plus the engine's pre-existing aliases kept for source compatibility:

  • BottomLeft/TopLeft ≡ WinUI BottomEdgeAlignedLeft/TopEdgeAlignedLeft.
  • BottomCenter/TopCenter ≡ WinUI Bottom/Top (center-justified).
  • BottomStretch = the attached ComboBox/AutoSuggest dropdown (flush, width-matched, corner-joined) — not a FlyoutBase mode; WinUI ComboBox places its own popup over/under the field.
InfoBadgeSeverity

The severity preset that picks an InfoBadge background and (for Icon badges) a default glyph, mirroring WinUI's {Attention,Informational,Success,Caution,Critical}InfoBadgeStyle set (InfoBadge_themeresources.xaml). Default == the plain accent badge.

InfoBarCloseReason

Why an InfoBar closed (WinUI InfoBarCloseReason): the user clicked the X (CloseButton) or it was closed in code / by the host (Programmatic).

InfoBarSeverity

The status level of an InfoBar, which selects its standard icon glyph and severity color (WinUI InfoBarSeverity). Default is Informational.

ItemContainerTrigger

How the user interacted with an ItemContainer — the WinUI ItemContainerInteractionTrigger subset the engine surfaces (ItemContainer.idl:17-26; PointerPressed/ PointerReleased collapse into Tap here — see Build(Element, bool, Action<bool>, bool, CornerRadius4?) remarks).

ItemsSelectionMode

WinUI ItemsViewSelectionMode (ItemsView.idl:6-12): None / Single / Multiple / Extended. ItemsView defaults to Single (ItemsView.h s_defaultSelectionMode).

MenuItemKind

The kind of a MenuFlyoutItem row: a plain command, a divider, a checkable ToggleMenuFlyoutItem (E73E check), a mutually-exclusive RadioMenuFlyoutItem (E915 bullet), or a cascading MenuFlyoutSubItem (E974 chevron + nested popup).

NavPaneDisplayMode

WinUI NavigationViewPaneDisplayMode (NavigationView.idl): Auto adapts by width; Left/LeftCompact/ LeftMinimal force a left-pane mode; Top renders the horizontal top bar with overflow.

NumberBoxSpinButtonPlacementMode

WinUI NumberBoxSpinButtonPlacementMode: where (if anywhere) the up/down spin buttons appear.

NumberBoxValidationMode

WinUI NumberBoxValidationMode: what happens to invalid / out-of-range input on commit.

OverlayCloseCause

Where an overlay close request came from; controls map this to their public close reason.

PaneMode
PasswordRevealMode

WinUI PasswordRevealMode (microsoft.ui.xaml.controls.controls.idl:163–167 — Peek, Hidden, Visible): Peek (default) = press-and-hold the reveal "eye" shows the password, release re-masks; Hidden = always masked, no reveal button; Visible = plain text always, no reveal button.

PipsPagerButtonVisibility

WinUI PipsPagerButtonVisibility (PipsPager.idl:6-11) — how the previous/next navigation buttons show. Both default to Collapsed (PipsPager.idl:56-59).

PopupChrome

Which presenter chrome + open/close MOTION the overlay host supplies around popup content. Each kind maps 1:1 to the WinUI transition family that owns that surface:

<ul><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Flyout" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — menus (MenuFlyout/DropDownButton/SplitButton/MenuBar/context): MenuPopupThemeTransition
(clip-reveal + content translate 250ms cubic-bezier(0,0,0,1); close = 83ms linear fade) — the default.</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Popup" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — the WinUI <code>Flyout</code>/FlyoutPresenter (FlyoutBase attaches PopupThemeTransition,
FlyoutBase_Partial.cpp:1968–1975): OS-PVL TAS_SHOWPOPUP/TAS_HIDEPOPUP (slide ±50 + delayed fade; close 83ms fade).</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Dropdown" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — the ComboBox dropdown: SplitOpen/SplitCloseThemeAnimation (generic.xaml:9047/9056) —
clip reveal 250ms with NO content translate and NO open fade; close = 167ms clip collapse + late 83ms fade. With
<xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupOptions.SeamOffsetY" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> set, the band is centred on the SEAM (selected row) and grows/shrinks both
ways (WinUI OffsetFromCenter); otherwise it reveals from the anchor edge.</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Raw" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — ToolTip/Slider value tip: FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation (TAS_FADEIN/TAS_FADEOUT, 167ms linear).</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Modal" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — ContentDialog scale/fade; <xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.TeachingTip" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — muxc expand/contract scale.</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Static" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — a bare WinUI <code>Popup</code> with no transitions (the AutoSuggestBox SuggestionsPopup,
generic.xaml AutoSuggestBox template — no TransitionCollection and AutoSuggestBox_Partial.cpp adds none): instant.</li></ul>
ProgressBarState

The drawing state of a ProgressBar — mirrors WinUI's CommonStates (Determinate / Paused / Error and their Indeterminate counterparts), selected from IsIndeterminate + ShowPaused + ShowError.

RepeatKind
SelectorVisual

The selector-VISUAL presets — the user-pickable item-chrome skins the premiere ItemsView exposes (the WinUI "ListView's accent bar" the user likes, GridView's corner check, ItemContainer's accent ring, …) as a single DATA enum so any layout × any selection mode can wear any selector (no WinUI-style capability cliffs):

  • AccentPill — the WinUI ListViewItem SelectionIndicator (the 3×16 accent bar at the row's left edge over a subtle backplate); the ListView preset's default.
  • Check — the WinUI GridViewItem dual-border + top-right corner check; the GridView preset's default.
  • FullRow — a documented SUPERSET (no exact WinUI analogue): the AccentPill row plate WITHOUT the left pill, so the whole row fills with the accent-subtle selected highlight (full-bleed selected backplate).
  • Border — the WinUI ItemContainer chrome (3px accent ring + 1px inner stroke + checkbox).
  • None — no selection chrome at all (a bare recyclable container) for app-drawn selection.
Slider.TickPlacement

Where tick marks render — WinUI TickPlacement (visibility mapping: Slider_Partial.cpp:2248-2303). WinUI's default is Inline (marks inside the rail, SliderInlineTickBarFill).

SplitViewDisplayMode

WinUI SplitViewDisplayMode (XCPTypesAutoGen Modules/Controls/SplitView/SplitView.cs:19-25). Overlay/CompactOverlay float the open pane ABOVE the content (no reflow) and are light-dismissible; Inline/CompactInline reserve layout width for it. Compact modes keep a CompactPaneLength rail visible while closed (the pane is CLIPPED to the rail, never resized — core SplitView.cpp:343-377 template settings).

SplitViewPanePlacement

WinUI SplitViewPanePlacement (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:11-15).

SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked

WinUI SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked (SwipeControl.idl:11-16). Auto and Close both close the control after the invoke (SwipeItem::InvokeSwipe closes on Close OR Auto — SwipeItem.cpp:40-45); RemainOpen keeps it revealed.

SwipeMode

WinUI SwipeMode (SwipeControl.idl:20-24): Reveal = resting tappable action buttons; Execute = ONE item stretched to the control width that auto-invokes when the swipe opens past the threshold.

TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode

WinUI TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode (TabView.idl:15-20). Auto/Always show the close button on every closable tab; OnPointerOver shows it only while the tab is selected or hovered (TabViewItem.cpp:251-282).

TabViewWidthMode

WinUI TabViewWidthMode (TabView.idl:6-11) — how tab headers size. Default Equal: every tab gets clamp(available/count, 100, 240) (TabView.cpp:1180-1228; TabViewItemMinWidth/MaxWidth, TabView_themeresources.xaml:243-244). SizeToContent: intrinsic. Compact: unselected tabs collapse to icon-only (TabViewItem.cpp:284-295; TabView.xaml:484-490).

TeachingTip.CloseReason

WinUI TeachingTipCloseReason — surfaced on Closing/Closed.

TeachingTip.HeroPlacement

WinUI TeachingTipHeroContentPlacementMode.

TeachingTip.PlacementMode

WinUI TeachingTipPlacementMode — all 13 modes are positioned (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates).

TeachingTip.TailVisibilityMode

WinUI TeachingTipTailVisibility.

TextChangeReason

Why the field's text changed — the WinUI AutoSuggestionBoxTextChangeReason shape, surfaced through LastChangeReason so AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox can split user edits from programmatic writes.

ToolTipPlacementMode

How the tooltip bubble is positioned — WinUI PlacementMode (the two modes ToolTipService actually drives): Top = centered above the TARGET, flipping on a collision (the WinUI default, ToolTip_Partial.cpp:1119-1122 "Fall back to the default - PlacementMode_Top"), the target rect inflated by the input-mode offset — 20px mouse / 12px keyboard (MousePlacementOffset); Mouse = at the last POINTER position, offset 11px below the cursor (ToolTip_Partial.h:56 m_mousePlacementVerticalOffset = 11; ToolTip_Partial.cpp:976-984 "align ToolTip with the bottom left corner of mouse bounding rectangle").

ViewboxStretch

How a Viewbox scales its child to the available box. Mirrors WinUI Stretch (default Uniform).

ViewboxStretchDirection

Constrains a Viewbox scale direction. Mirrors WinUI StretchDirection (default Both).

Delegates

ItemContainerFactory

Custom item-container factory — the E11-L4 SKIN seam: the List/Grid presets + TreeView supply their WinUI item chrome (ListViewItemPresenter / GridView dual-border / TreeViewItem row) around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate. The returned BoxEl must wire onInteraction (press/Enter/Space → the selector) and onFocusChanged (keyboard-current tracking), and should be Focusable so the engine focus ring lands on items. Null ⇒ the default WinUI ItemContainer chrome.

ReorderCrossCommit

The ONE cross-list commit callback (plan E5-L3): the app removes the item from from at fromIndex and inserts payload into to at toIndex — one atomic model mutation, both lists re-render from it (signals-first). from is null (and fromIndex −1) when the drag came from a plain BoxEl.Draggable of the shared kind rather than another Reorderable (a deposit-style insert).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
